questions.question: Which of the following correctly declares and creates an int array of size 5 in Java?
What is the default value of elements in a newly created int[] array?
What is the default value of elements in a newly created String[] array?
What is the default value of elements in a newly created boolean[] array?
What is the default value of elements in a newly created double[] array?
Which of the following uses literal initialization to create an array?
Given int[] arr = {10, 20, 30, 40, 50}; what does arr[0] return?
options: An error because arrays start at index 1

questions.question: Given int[] arr = {10, 20, 30, 40, 50}; which expression accesses the last element?
How do you get the number of elements in an array named data?
Why does the array length use arr.length instead of arr.length()?
options: It is a bug in Java that was never fixed
length is a public field (property), not a method
length() is only for ArrayList, not arrays
Both arr.length and arr.length() work the same way
questions.answer: length is a public field (property), not a method

questions.question: What exception is thrown when you access an array index that does not exist?
Given int[] arr = {10, 20, 30}; what happens when you execute System.out.println(arr[3]);?
Given int[] arr = {10, 20, 30}; what happens when you execute System.out.println(arr[-1]);?
Which loop is best suited for iterating through an array when you need the index of each element?
What is the correct syntax for an enhanced for loop over int[] scores?
What is true about arrays in Java?
options: Arrays can grow or shrink after creation
Arrays are objects with a fixed size once created
Arrays do not have a length property
questions.answer: Arrays are objects with a fixed size once created

questions.question: Given int[] a = {1, 2, 3}; int[] b = a; b[0] = 99; what is the value of a[0]?
Why does assigning one array variable to another (int[] b = a;) NOT create a copy?
options: Both variables point to (reference) the same array object in memory
The assignment operator copies only the first element
Java does not support the assignment operator for arrays
questions.answer: Both variables point to (reference) the same array object in memory

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid way to create a true copy of an array?
When an array is passed to a method in Java, what is passed?
options: A copy of the entire array
A reference to the array object
A read-only version of the array
questions.answer: A reference to the array object

questions.question: Given the method: public static void doubleAll(int[] arr) { for (int i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) { arr[i] *= 2; } } After calling doubleAll(myArray), what happens to myArray?
options: myArray is unchanged because Java passes arrays by value
myArray is unchanged because the method creates a local copy
All elements in myArray are doubled because the method received a reference
questions.answer: All elements in myArray are doubled because the method received a reference

questions.question: In bubble sort, what does each pass through the array accomplish?
options: It finds the minimum element and places it at the beginning
It compares adjacent elements and swaps them if they are in the wrong order
It divides the array into two halves and merges them
It selects a pivot element and partitions the array
questions.answer: It compares adjacent elements and swaps them if they are in the wrong order

questions.question: Which import statement is needed to use Arrays.copyOf() and Arrays.sort()?
options: No import is needed; Arrays is in java.lang

questions.question: A program uses a large array and a separate counter variable to track how many elements are actually filled. What limitation of arrays does this approach work around?
options: Arrays have a fixed size and cannot grow dynamically
Arrays cannot be passed to methods
questions.answer: Arrays have a fixed size and cannot grow dynamically

questions.question: What is the correct standard for loop condition to iterate through every element of an array named arr without going out of bounds?
